mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces real savings growth, it indicates demand is being met by supply without corresponding household liquidity, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In periods where housing starts exceed 1.5 million annualized units, increased construction activity boosts consumer confidence and durable goods demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high housing start periods are statistically indistinguishable from baseline returns (p > 0.10), or the signal reverses direction during the 2008-2009 financial crisis window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding real economic activity, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0 percent, borrowing costs suppress corporate investment and consumer spending, causing a structural drag on equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite rates exceeding 5.0 percent due to alternative liquidity drivers, or the signal produces a positive average forward return over a 2-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 200,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls, it confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle that fuels wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive risk premium over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient becomes negative during periods of rapid rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain stress episodes where industrial production declines while CPI remains elevated, profit margins face simultaneous demand destruction and cost push, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation/low-production periods are not significantly negative compared to baseline, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over a single CPI or INDPRO signal.
